artifacts.content:
Subject: Front sign, Hilux mess, and Dad’s truck

Hey Dad,

Sitting in the office at 18 Ironstone Drive waiting for a parts delivery, thought I’d finally send you an update on the front of the workshop.

I’ve decided the big Pilbara Desert Mechanical pylon sign is going in that front landscaped strip, about 2.5 metres back from the Ironstone Drive fence and just left of the driveway. It’s pretty much where we used to park that white Hilux before Westpac Business sent the repo truck in August 2023 when I was three months behind on the $820‑a‑fortnight repayments after I lost those two fleet contracts. There are still old oil stains there from when the towies hooked it up, so I’m half doing it to cover the marks and stop blokes asking why “the finance mob” were out the front that morning.

The spot also lines up so the deep red and charcoal grey sign will help block the view into the side yard where your old Kenworth prime mover is parked up. Since your mild stroke in June 2022 and having to give up the Dubbo–Pilbara run, customers keep asking what’s wrong with “Pete’s truck” and why it’s just sitting there. With the sign and those 1‑metre shrubs, people will see the business branding instead of a retired rig.

Ben reckons he can help me run the power when he’s next up from Perth. I’m going with the aluminium composite panels and bright orange logo highlights like we used on the spray booth after the fuel-line fire last September – looks sharp and ties in with the shed.

How’s the hip holding up around Dubbo? Lucas still talks about your big trips.

Cheers,
Jordan

metadata.subject: Front sign, Hilux mess, and Dad’s truck

artifacts.content:
Subject: Hand, hoist drama & physio – trying to be sensible

Hey Em,

Just grabbed five minutes in the office before I head over to Newman Hospital for the Thursday arvo physio. Still booked for 3:30 p.m. every Thursday while Dr. Helen Carter keeps me on this return‑to‑work plan after the March 2024 hoist stuff‑up.

I was looking out at Bay 2 this morning where the tow truck dumped the Ranger the day I crushed those two fingers. Can’t believe that was only last March – one second I’m lining up the arms, next second I’m on the ground swearing and the ambos are calling Hedland Health Campus about a transfer. The scar tissue still pulls when I try to swing a gearbox around.

I’ve told the sign bloke we’re keeping the new pylon sign out in the front garden strip, about 2.5 m inside the Ironstone Drive boundary, away from Bay 2. I can manage light duties – marking out the post centres, checking levels – but I don’t want to be digging footings right at the workshop doors while the hand’s still dodgy.

Lights-wise I’m keeping it tame. After that near‑miss with the road train on Great Northern Highway on 14 March 2023 and the blackout/migraine auras, Dr. Patel at Newman Hospital warned me about bright LEDs. So I told them to keep the sign’s LED intensity around what we trialled on the façade in August 2023 – roughly that 90‑watt low‑glare setup – nothing harsher.

Kids okay for pickup from Newman Primary and kindy this arvo? I can swing past Woolies for milk on the way back from physio if you need.

Love,
Jord

metadata.subject: Hand, hoist drama & physio – trying to be sensible

artifacts.content:
Title: Sign materials / compliance / money headaches

- Location: Front landscaped strip at 18 Ironstone Dr, ~2.5 m inside boundary, centred on lot frontage. Same area I stand to watch for Lucas’s Newman Primary bus now, after that scare in Feb 2024 when he slipped through the gate towards Ironstone Drive while I was yarning with the Rio Tinto supervisor. New pylon + trimmed shrubs should help funnel him back toward the shed, not the driveway.

- Structure: Freestanding, double‑sided pylon on two 150 x 150 mm hot‑dip gal SHS posts, 900 mm into reinforced concrete pads (600 x 600 mm). Learned from Dad’s fall on oily concrete at the Dubbo depot in May 2022 and his hip replacement at Dubbo Base – broom finish around bases + non‑slip sealer from Dulux Trade Centre Port Hedland so no one stacks it walking under the sign.

- Materials: 3 mm ACP with UV‑stable vinyl graphics + anti‑graffiti laminate. Same spec as the RAC WA assessor signed off on after the 12 March 2024 break‑in at Pilbara Desert Mechanical. Mounting near, but not onto, the steel security grill the cops from Newman Station had us weld over the front.

- LEDs: Low‑voltage modules with photoelectric sensor + lockable isolator inside workshop. Don’t repeat the October 2022 Main Roads WA fine for the unlit temporary banner on Great Northern Highway.

- Cost control: Still two months behind on BAS/ATO payment plan from August 2023 – reuse leftover SHS from hoist install Ben wired in July 2023 and that half‑price pallet of slightly warped ACP from Bunnings Port Hedland.

Remember: keep bright orange accents minimal – avoid “debt collection” vibe like the May 2023 overdue‑accounts flyers.

metadata.title: Sign materials / compliance / money headaches
metadata.location: Pilbara Desert Mechanical – office whiteboard copy into OneNote

artifacts.content:
Subject: Loans, colours and trying to think long term

Hey Ben,

Thanks again for talking me through the numbers after hours last week. My head’s still spinning from that March 2024 refinance meeting with Mark Ellison at NAB Newman. Fifteen‑year term now, so the loan runs out around 2040, which basically means I need Pilbara Desert Mechanical and that big front sign working hard for at least the next 15 years.

I’ve booked a bloke in Perth who did the rebrand after the January 2024 small claims mess with the old contractor over the previous logo. He’s keeping the deep red and charcoal grey with bright orange highlights we settled on in March. I like that it quietly nods to Dad’s PTSD awareness livery we put on his Kenworth back in Dubbo – same deep red/charcoal combo and the orange like the ribbon he got at the Dubbo RSL fundraiser in January 2022.

Feels weird thinking that by the time this sign has had its 10–15 years, Lucas will be finishing Year 12 at Newman Senior High around late 2034 and the DHS child support setup with Sarah Collins in Dubbo will be done. Part of me wonders if that’s when we look at heading back east, depending how Dad’s Parkinson’s is going.

Anyway, practical stuff: when you’re next up, I need you to certify the low‑voltage LED setup and the lockable isolator so we don’t have another Main Roads‑style drama. Also want your eyes on the load from tapping the sign into the board you re‑did after the August 2022 electrical fire on the side wall.

Say hi to Claire and the kids.

Cheers,
Jord

metadata.subject: Loans, colours and trying to think long term

artifacts.content:
Title: Thursday run – physio, sign check, Lucas bus

Date: Thursday 2025-03-13
Time: 14:30–17:30
Location: Start/finish – Pilbara Desert Mechanical, 18 Ironstone Dr
Attendees: Jordan

Agenda / notes:

14:30–15:00 – Quick tidy of front landscaped strip (~2.5 m inside Ironstone Drive boundary, north of driveway). Check lockbox with spare keys + breathalyser is still hidden behind shrubs. That’s the one Emily and I set up in November 2023 after I blew 0.06 on the random breath test just past the Newman Hotel – use it before any late‑night call‑outs.

Mark out final footing positions for freestanding double‑sided pylon sign (150 x 150 SHS posts, 900 mm deep pads). Keep clear of Bay 2 so I’m not tempted to overdo it with the hand.

15:00–15:20 – Drive to Newman Hospital.

15:30–16:00 – Weekly hand physio (return‑to‑work) – outpatient clinic, Newman Hospital. Still part of the plan Dr. Helen Carter set up after the March 2024 hoist incident in Bay 2 when I crushed two fingers and ended up getting flown to Hedland Health Campus.

16:00–16:20 – Drive back to workshop.

16:20–17:00 – Wait by front fence (same central strip where new sign will sit) to watch for Lucas’s bus from Newman Primary. Remember Feb 2024 near‑miss when he slipped through the gate towards Ironstone while I was talking to the Rio Tinto supervisor – use cones/sign to funnel him back toward shed.

17:00–17:30 – Close up, check LED trial board brightness against migraine limits Dr. Patel mentioned.

metadata.title: Thursday run – physio, sign check, Lucas bus

artifacts.content:
Subject: Zoe’s ears, fence sign and finally doing this properly

Hi Mum, Dad,

Just a quick update while I’m hiding in the office out of the heat.

You’ll remember back in early June 2021 when we had to cancel the big fascia sign I’d booked with Newman Print & Signs because we suddenly needed about $4,800 for the gap on Zoe’s ear surgery at Perth Children’s Hospital. That’s when I knocked up that little 1.2 m x 0.6 m metal panel and cable‑tied it to the inside of the chain‑mesh fence near the driveway at 18 Ironstone Drive. It even had the old after‑hours number that rang straight through to your landline in Dubbo – sorry again for all the late calls back then.

Since then that poor sign’s had a few lives. I took it down around the time Emily’s mum was staying with us for her Family Court stuff in Perth, after the solicitor at Tindall Gask Bentley suggested it was better not to advertise a home‑based workshop so loudly. Put it back up in February 2022 once things settled, then pulled it off again for a few months when the CAMHS social worker from Newman Hospital was coming weekly to see Lucas about his anxiety – didn’t want the place looking like a busy truck yard while he was struggling.

Now that the proper freestanding pylon sign is nearly ready, I’m planning to remove that old fence panel for good – cable ties, light‑gauge wire, everything – and leave the chain‑mesh neat with no sharp bits.

Kids are good – Zoe’s hearing checks have been fine, and Lucas is back into footy.

Love,
Jordan

metadata.subject: Zoe’s ears, fence sign and finally doing this properly

artifacts.content:
Title: Long game – business, back, and when to call it

Dumping some thoughts so I don’t lose them.

Realistically, the big illuminated sign out the front of Pilbara Desert Mechanical needs to earn its keep for at least the next 10–15 years. Between the chronic back issues from the L4–L5 disc herniation that flared after that 12‑hour breakdown call‑out on the Great Northern Highway in May 2022, and what Dr. James O’Reilly at Newman Hospital keeps warning me about, I know I won’t be wrenching full‑time forever. But for now, plan is: keep the sign and business solid for the next decade or so while we knock down the NAB refinance Mark Ellison set up in March 2024.

That loan runs about 15 years, to roughly 2040, which lines up weirdly neatly with other stuff: Lucas finishing Year 12 at Newman Senior High around late 2034 and the DHS child support arrangement with Sarah Collins ending; Dad’s Parkinson’s likely getting worse so Emily and I might be looking at moving back to Dubbo somewhere around 2036–2038 (Dr. Karen Patel at Newman Family Medical Centre reckons he’ll need more hands‑on help by then).

So: sign lifespan = business horizon. Deep red/charcoal/bright orange branding stays consistent – same as the May 2023 overdue‑accounts flyers and the March 2024 rebrand after the small‑claims logo drama. Keep lighting intensity at the softer level from the 1 July 2023 “One Year Accident Free” barbie banner so we don’t repeat Jake’s eye‑strain comp claim.

If the back or Dad’s health goes downhill faster than that, reassess.

J.

metadata.title: Long game – business, back, and when to call it
